title: Eviction Prevention Assistance - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Eviction Prevention Assistance 16 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter AIDS/HIV Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF 211INFO MULTNOMAH COUNTY EVICTION PREVENTION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF May be able to provide financial or legal assistance with non-payment of rent evictions for households with an eviction notice. Cannot assist with evictions issued due to property damage, illegal activity, or creating a nuisance. Assistance is not guaranteed and is determined on a case-by-case basis. There may be a waitlist and assistance is not same day or immediate. Partnering agencies work to assist households as fast as possible. Eligibility: Household with any type of eviction notice for non-payment of rent (72 hour, 10 day, 30 day, court ordered, notice of restitution) Hours: Phone, Text, Email: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details CASCADE AIDS PROJECT CAP HOUSING SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides case management to help individuals find housing and related resources. Services include short- and long-term housing search support and one-time emergency rental assistance for security deposits or eviction prevention. 520 NW Davis Street Suite 215 Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV/AIDS Email (503) 223-5907 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES EUGENE HOMELESSNESS PREVENTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers eviction prevention services, which may include help with current or past-due rent and utilities (like electricity, water, gas, garbage, sewer, or heating fuel), landlord support, and case management. Services depend on available funding and are not guaranteed. 1464 W 6th Avenue Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Must be a current tenant who is unstably housed or at imminent risk of homelessness Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 8am-4pm, Thursday 9am-4pm Email (541) 345-3628 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES SPRINGFIELD HOMELESSNESS PREVENTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers eviction prevention services, which may include help with current or past-due rent and utilities (like electricity, water, gas, garbage, sewer, or heating fuel), landlord support, and case management. Services depend on available funding and are not guaranteed. 1025 G Street Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Must be a current tenant who is unstably housed or at imminent risk of homelessness Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 8am-4pm, Thursday 9am-4pm Email (541) 345-3628 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY SHARING PROGRAM HOMELESSNESS PREVENTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers eviction prevention services, which may include help with current or past-due rent and utilities (like electricity, water, gas, garbage, sewer, or heating fuel), landlord support, and case management. Services depend on available funding and are not guaranteed. 1440 Birch Avenue Cottage Grove, OR 97424 Eligibility: Must be a current tenant who is unstably housed or at imminent risk of homelessness Hours: Monday 1:30pm-6pm, Tuesday-Friday 11am-3:30pm Email (541) 942-2176 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EL PROGRAMA HISPANO CATÓLICO FAIR HOUSING ENFORCEMENT COLLABORATIVE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about tenant rights and refers cases requiring legal advice or representation to attorneys at Legal Aid Services of Oregon, Fair Housing Council of Oregon, and Oregon Law Center. Also includes culturally-specific case workers located in geographically underserved areas. Also conducts outreach and tenant rights education in the community. 333 SE 223rd Avenue Suite 100 Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (503) 669-8350 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES NEW ROADS HOMELESSNESS PREVENTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers eviction prevention services, which may include help with current or past-due rent and utilities (like electricity, water, gas, garbage, sewer, or heating fuel), landlord support, and case management. Services depend on available funding and are not guaranteed. 941 W 7th Avenue Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Youth up to age 24. Must be a current tenant who is unstably housed or at imminent risk of homelessness. Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday/Saturday/Sunday 8:30am-4:45pm, Wednesday 8:30am-2pm and 3:30pm-4:45pm Email (541) 686-4310 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON COAST COMMUNITY ACTION OREGON COAST COMMUNITY ACTION COOS HOUSING STABILIZATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers coordinated entry services for housing. Assistance may include help with rent deposits, rental assistance, or eviction prevention. Households may receive one-time assistance within a 12-month period. Assistance depends on eligibility, funding, and program requirements. 1855 Thomas Avenue Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: To be considered, households must generally: Meet income eligibility requirements. Income limits vary by funding source and are usually based on Area Median Income (AMI). Be experiencing a housing crisis, such as: being homeless OR being at risk of homelessness (for example, an eviction notice) OR actively fleeing domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, or stalking. (Documentation is required.) Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON COAST COMMUNITY ACTION OREGON COAST COMMUNITY ACTION CURRY HOUSING STABILIZATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers coordinated entry services for housing. Assistance may include help with rent deposits, rental assistance, or eviction prevention. Households may receive one-time assistance within a 12-month period. Assistance depends on eligibility, funding, and program requirements. 937 Chetco Avenue Suite D Brookings, OR 97415 Eligibility: To be considered, households must generally: Meet income eligibility requirements. Income limits vary by funding source and are usually based on Area Median Income (AMI). Be experiencing a housing crisis, such as: being homeless OR being at risk of homelessness (for example, an eviction notice) OR actively fleeing domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, or stalking. (Documentation is required.) Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW CENTER COOS BAY REGIONAL OFFICE GENERAL LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal counseling and/or representation for low-income individuals who need assistance with routine legal matters (civil, non-criminal), usually regarding housing, public benefits, family, or elder law. Some locations may offer additional services. 490 N 2nd Street Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Low-income individuals who are at or below 125% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL) Hours: Walk in: Monday/Wednesday 1:30pm-4pm Telephone hours only: Tuesday/Thursday 1:30pm-4pm (541) 269-1226 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW CENTER LANE COUNTY LEGAL AID GENERAL LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides general legal counseling and/or representation for low-income individuals who need assistance with routine legal matters (civil, non-criminal), usually regarding housing, public benefits, family, or elder law. Some locations may offer additional services. 101 E Broadway Suite 200 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Low-income individuals who are at or below 125% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL) Hours: Office hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 9am-11:45am and 12:45pm-5pm Intake for new clients: Monday/Wednesday/Friday 1pm-3pm (541) 485-1017 100 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW CENTER PORTLAND REGIONAL OFFICE OREGON LAW HELP WEBSITE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Website offers legal information regarding various issues, including housing, family law, employment law, elder law, government benefits, and consumer protection. Provides links to legal service providers, resources, and a directory of legal aid providers. 621 SW Morrison Street Suite 1450 Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW CENTER STATEWIDE EVICTION DEFENSE PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal assistance and representation to tenants facing eviction court cases. Must contact agency before first court appearance date. Eligibility: Low-income households who have an eviction or vacate notice, and are at 125% Federal Poverty Level (FPL) or less (some exceptions for those slightly above, on a case-by case basis) Hours: Monday-Wednesday 9am-1pm Email (888) 585-9638 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SIUSLAW OUTREACH SERVICES SIUSLAW AREA RESOURCE CENTER HOMELESSNESS PREVENTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers eviction prevention services, which may include help with current or past-due rent and utilities (like electric, water, gas, garbage, sewer, or heating fuel), landlord support, and case management. Services depend on available funding and are not guaranteed. 1536 W 12th Street Florence, OR 97439 Eligibility: Must be a current tenant who is unstably housed or at imminent risk of homelessness Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-3:30pm, Friday 10am-4pm Email (541) 997-2816 Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE COMMONS LAW CENTER TENANT EVICTION DEFENSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides tenants with brief advice, helps negotiate agreements with their landlord's lawyers, and facilitates rental assistance applications. Also offers in-person eviction defense for tenants with an eviction court case during the morning docket at Multnomah and East County Courthouses. PO Box 14280 Portland, OR 97293 Eligibility: Below 400% of the federal poverty level. Most services are limited to tenants served at the Multnomah County Courthouse clinic during the first appearance of their eviction case but citizenship and legal residency are not required. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm In-Person Eviction Court Defense at the following locations: Multnomah County Courthouse 1200 SW 1st Avenue, Portland, OR 97204, Monday/Tuesday/Thursday 8am-noon East County Courthouse 18480 SE Stark Street, Portland, OR 97233, Wednesday 8am-noon Email (503) 850-0811 Get Directions Visit Website More Details URBAN LEAGUE OF PORTLAND FAIR HOUSING ENFORCEMENT COLLABORATIVE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about tenant rights, eviction prevention, and legal assistance, referring cases requiring legal advice or representation by attorneys to Legal Aid Services of Oregon, Fair Housing Council of Oregon, and Oregon Law Center. Also, conducts outreach and tenant rights education in the community. Also includes culturally-specific case workers located in geographically underserved areas. 3934 NE Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Portland, OR 97212 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 280-2600 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASCADE AIDS PROJECT CAP HOUSING SERVICES CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY HOMELESSNESS PREVENTION CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY HOMELESSNESS PREVENTION COMMUNITY SHARING PROGRAM HOMELESSNESS PREVENTION EL PROGRAMA HISPANO CATÓLICO FAIR HOUSING ENFORCEMENT COLLABORATIVE LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES HOMELESSNESS PREVENTION OREGON COAST COMMUNITY ACTION HOUSING STABILIZATION SERVICES OREGON COAST COMMUNITY ACTION HOUSING STABILIZATION SERVICES OREGON LAW CENTER GENERAL LEGAL AID OREGON LAW CENTER GENERAL LEGAL AID OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW HELP WEBSITE SIUSLAW OUTREACH SERVICES HOMELESSNESS PREVENTION THE COMMONS LAW CENTER TENANT EVICTION DEFENSE URBAN LEAGUE OF PORTLAND FAIR HOUSING ENFORCEMENT COLLABORATIVE
